newsSinceJun6: No material company-specific news since June 6. The latest notable items - the June 3 ADH series 800 V DC-DC converter launch for AI data centers (up to 8 kW peak, megawatt-rack capable with NDQ/Hot Swap modules) and the June 3 BofA Global Technology Conference presentation (mgmt targeting low-to-mid 20s% 2026 revenue growth, data center mid-30s%) - predate the window and were already reflected in yesterday's hold. Today's -0.71% is noise.

sources.label: Business Wire - Advanced Energy Introduces 800 V DC Converters for Next-Generation AI Data Centers (Jun 3, 2026)
Seeking Alpha - AEIS presents at Bank of America 2026 Global Technology Conference (Jun 3, 2026)
StockTitan - Advanced Energy debuts 800V DC AI data center converters

verdicts.rankRationale: Hold at rank 57: no new information since June 6 and a flat tape (-0.7% today) leave yesterday's setup unchanged - the 800V AI rack-power optionality is real but the +37% YTD rerating and $12B mid-cap visibility mean residual alpha is already fairly captured in the Monitor/benchmark bucket.
verdicts.bull: ADH 800V DC-DC platform positions AEIS squarely in the NVIDIA-led 800 VDC rack-architecture transition, and management's mid-30s% data-center growth target implies the AI power story still has legs.
verdicts.neutral: A well-run power-conversion compounder with genuine AI exposure, but at +37% YTD and broadly bullish sell-side coverage it is no longer a hidden bottleneck name; ranks appropriately mid-pack on a residual-alpha list.
verdicts.bear: Valuation screens stretched (GF Value flags heavy overvaluation), semi-equipment power is cyclical, and the 800V transition invites competition from Vicor, Delta, and Vertiv-aligned suppliers that could compress the AI power-density premium.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Hyperscaler design wins or volume orders for the ADH 800V platform; next earnings print confirming mid-30s% data-center growth.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Multiple compression on any data-center order-rate wobble after a 37% YTD run with no fresh fundamental evidence since early June.
